rag_rules: Place AGREE assumptions and guarantees in component type scopes when they refer to visible ports or features.
Use implementation scopes for bindings and assignments that involve subcomponent connections or outputs.
Ensure all identifiers in AGREE annexes are visible in the current AADL component scope.
generic_rules: AGREE contracts must be placed in appropriate AADL scopes: type annexes for component-level contracts, implementation annexes for binding-level details.
Only reference visible AADL features and declared helpers in AGREE annexes to avoid unresolved identifier errors.
Avoid using implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type contracts; move such bindings to implementation annexes.
Validate that all AGREE types (int, real, bool) match the expected AADL data types to prevent type mismatch errors.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annex blocks are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};`.
Assumptions and guarantees use `assume "label": expression;` and `guarantee "label": expression;`.
Equation declarations use `eq name : type = expression;` and constant declarations use `const name : type = value;`.
Assignments use `assign output = expression;` and are valid only in implementation scopes.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type annexes unless they are valid outputs or assignments.
Do not reference package-qualified identifiers or AADL properties that are not visible in the current scope.
Do not assume that AADL ports or data types are automatically available in AGREE expression scope without explicit declaration.
irrelevant_topics: Model analyst extraction of AADL structure
AGREE syntax and scope notes for model fusion
